title: Dynamic Hashing and Priority Queue Integration
description: A flowchart showing how dynamic hashing manages storage for a priority queue, enabling efficient insertion, deletion, and access to the highest priority element.
relationships: Hash function maps a record's key to a bucket address for storage
Insert operation adds a record to both its hashed bucket and the priority queue
The priority queue's heap structure maintains the highest priority element at the root
Extract-Min removes the root element from the queue and its corresponding record from the hash table
Dynamic hashing resizes buckets to manage load and maintain efficient access
